spanish: Antes de firmar el contrato, necesitamos verificar que todas las cláusulas cumplan con las regulaciones locales.

text: We have reached an agreement on all key points; let's draft the final contract and sign it {{c1::tomorrow}}.
explanation: 'Tomorrow' is the day after today, commonly set as a target date in contract negotiations once terms are aligned.
usage:
Pattern: sign it tomorrow / schedule for tomorrow
  • The documents will be ready for signing tomorrow morning.
  • Let's meet tomorrow to review the feedback.
spanish: Hemos llegado a un acuerdo en todos los puntos clave; redactemos el contrato final y firmémoslo mañana.

text: We are willing to compromise on the delivery method if you can cover the shipping {{c1::costs}}.
explanation: 'Shipping costs' are the expenses required to package and transport physical goods from the seller to the buyer.
usage:
Pattern: shipping costs / freight charges
  • The online price does not include shipping costs.
  • We offer free shipping costs for orders over $500.
spanish: Estamos dispuestos a ceder en el método de entrega si usted puede cubrir los costos de envío.

text: Let's review the termination clause: what is the notice period required to end the contract without {{c1::penalties}}?
explanation: A 'penalty' is a punishment or financial fine imposed by contract terms for failing to comply with a clause (such as early termination).
usage:
Pattern: contract penalties / cancellation fee
  • The contract includes financial penalties for late delivery.
  • You can cancel the service after one year without penalties.
spanish: Revisemos la cláusula de rescisión: ¿cuál es el período de notificación requerido para finalizar el contrato sin penalizaciones?

text: Let's {{c1::touch base}} tomorrow to discuss the updates.
explanation: touch base is a common business idiom meaning to briefly contact or meet with someone to talk about progress.
usage:
Pattern: touch base with [someone] [time]
  • I need to touch base with my manager before the client call.
  • Let's touch base next week.
spanish: Póngamonos en contacto mañana para discutir las actualizaciones.

text: We need to {{c1::push back}} the meeting to 3 PM.
explanation: push back means to postpone or reschedule an event to a later time or date.
usage:
Pattern: push back [event] to [time/date]
  • Can we push back the project deadline by two days?
  • They had to push back the launch due to technical issues.
spanish: Necesitamos posponer la reunión para las 3 PM.

text: I'll {{c1::follow up with}} you via email once I get the data.
explanation: follow up with means to contact someone again to provide further information, verify progress, or request updates.
usage:
Pattern: follow up with [someone] on/via [method]
  • The doctor will follow up with you next month.
  • I will follow up with the client on the contract details tomorrow.
spanish: Le daré seguimiento por correo electrónico una vez que obtenga los datos.

text: During the planning sync, the director shared: "Our {{c1::financial forecast}} projects new {{c2::revenue streams}} from our enterprise API licensing model."
explanation: Financial forecast is a projection of future financial performance. **Revenue streams** refer to different sources of income for a business.
usage:
Pattern: financial forecast projects [metric] / identify new revenue streams
  • The financial forecast projects a 15% increase in sales.
  • We are exploring new revenue streams to diversify our income.
spanish: Durante la sincronización de planificación, el director compartió: "Nuestro pronóstico financiero proyecta nuevas fuentes de ingresos a partir de nuestro modelo de licencia de API empresarial".

text: Could you please take notes during the presentation and share them with the team {{c1::afterwards}}?
explanation: 'Afterwards' is an adverb meaning at a later or subsequent time; after something else has occurred.
usage:
Pattern: afterwards / after the meeting
  • We had a brief discussion afterwards.
  • She completed the task and left immediately afterwards.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor tomar notas durante la presentación y compartirlas con el equipo después/posteriormente?

text: Let's go around the table and have everyone briefly introduce themselves and their {{c1::area of expertise}}.
explanation: An 'area of expertise' is the specific field, subject, or skill set in which a person is highly knowledgeable or skilled.
usage:
Pattern: area of expertise / domain knowledge
  • Database performance tuning is my main area of expertise.
  • We hired consultants with expertise in cloud migration.
spanish: Hagamos una ronda en la mesa para que todos se presenten brevemente y mencionen su área de especialización.

text: I'd like to share my screen to show you the latest mockup designs for the {{c1::landing page}}.
explanation: A 'landing page' is the specific web page that a user arrives at after clicking a link or advertisement, designed to convert visitors.
usage:
Pattern: landing page / homepage
  • We optimized the landing page to increase sign-ups.
  • The marketing campaign directs traffic to a custom landing page.
spanish: Me gustaría compartir mi pantalla para mostrarles los últimos diseños de maqueta para la página de destino (inicio).

text: Let's reserve the last ten minutes of the meeting for a quick Q&A session to address any {{c1::remaining questions}}.
explanation: A 'Q&A session' (Question and Answer) is a dedicated time during a meeting or presentation for the audience to ask questions of the speaker.
usage:
Pattern: Q&A session / questions and answers
  • The webinar ended with a lively Q&A session.
  • We will take questions during the Q&A session at the end.
spanish: Reservemos los últimos diez minutos de la reunión para una sesión rápida de preguntas y respuestas para abordar cualquier pregunta restante.

text: I agree with your suggestion, let's incorporate that feedback into the next iteration of the {{c1::proposal}}.
explanation: A 'proposal' is a formal written document or offer presented to others for consideration, typically detailing a plan, budget, or contract.
usage:
Pattern: project proposal / business proposal
  • We submitted our proposal to the client yesterday.
  • The board will review the proposal at the next meeting.
spanish: Estoy de acuerdo con su sugerencia, incorporemos esa retroalimentación en la próxima iteración de la propuesta.

text: Before we begin, let's quickly review the {{c1::agenda items}} to make sure we allocate our time properly.
explanation: Agenda items are the individual topics or tasks scheduled to be discussed during a meeting.
usage:
Pattern: agenda items / meeting agenda
  • We have five agenda items to cover today.
  • Please add this topic to the agenda items for next week.
spanish: Antes de comenzar, revisemos rápidamente los puntos de la agenda para asegurarnos de asignar nuestro tiempo adecuadamente.

text: Could you please take minutes during the meeting and email them to the attendees {{c1::by tomorrow morning}}?
explanation: 'By tomorrow morning' is a common business deadline, requesting that a task be completed and sent before the start of the next workday.
usage:
Pattern: by tomorrow morning / first thing tomorrow
  • I'll have the code review ready by tomorrow morning.
  • Please send the contract to the client by tomorrow morning.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor tomar la minuta durante la reunión y enviarla por correo electrónico a los asistentes para mañana por la mañana?

text: Let's introduce our new team member, Colin, who will be taking over the database migration {{c1::project}}.
explanation: A 'project' is an individual or collaborative enterprise that is carefully planned and designed to achieve a particular aim.
usage:
Pattern: project manager / project milestones
  • I am managing the new API development project.
  • The database migration project was completed on schedule.
spanish: Presentemos a nuestro nuevo miembro del equipo, Colin, quien se hará cargo del proyecto de migración de la base de datos.

text: I'd like to present the user flow diagram to show how the customer registers on our {{c1::platform}}.
explanation: A 'platform' is a major software environment, website, or infrastructure where services are hosted and executed.
usage:
Pattern: software platform / cloud platform
  • Our platform supports over 10,000 concurrent connections.
  • We deployed our application on a secure hosting platform.
spanish: Me gustaría presentar el diagrama de flujo de usuario para mostrar cómo el cliente se registra en nuestra plataforma.

text: We will allocate the last fifteen minutes of the meeting for an open discussion to address any {{c1::concerns}}.
explanation: A 'concern' is a matter of interest, anxiety, or worry raised by team members or clients regarding a plan or change.
usage:
Pattern: address concerns / voice concerns
  • The director met with staff to address their concerns about the restructuring.
  • If you have any safety concerns, please voice them immediately.
spanish: Asignaremos los últimos quince minutos de la reunión para una discusión abierta para abordar cualquier preocupación.

text: I agree with your proposal; let's schedule a follow-up call to plan the implementation {{c1::steps}}.
explanation: 'Steps' refer to the phases or individual tasks required to carry out a proposal or plan.
usage:
Pattern: implementation steps / action steps
  • The document outlines the implementation steps for the database migration.
  • We followed the configuration steps exactly.
spanish: Estoy de acuerdo con su propuesta; programemos una llamada de seguimiento para planificar los pasos de implementación.

text: Please make sure you have the report ready before the meeting starts so we can reference the {{c1::metrics}}.
explanation: 'Metrics' are measures of quantitative assessment commonly used for assessing, comparing, and tracking performance (e.g. latency, conversions).
usage:
Pattern: performance metrics / tracking metrics
  • Our primary performance metric is API response time.
  • Review the marketing metrics before launching the campaign.
spanish: Por favor, asegúrese de tener el informe listo antes de que comience la reunión para que podamos hacer referencia a las métricas.

text: If there are no objections, let's proceed with the plan and assign tasks to the respective team {{c1::members}}.
explanation: A 'member' is an individual belonging to a group or team.
usage:
Pattern: team members / staff members
  • All team members must attend the daily stand-up meeting.
  • We welcomed three new members to our division.
spanish: Si no hay objeciones, procedamos con el plan y asignemos tareas a los respectivos miembros del equipo.

text: I'd like to share a quick update on our customer acquisition costs for this past {{c1::quarter}}.
explanation: A 'quarter' (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) is a three-month period in a financial calendar, used to report business earnings and metrics.
usage:
Pattern: fiscal quarter / third quarter / Q3
  • Our revenue increased by 10% in the last quarter.
  • We are planning our goals for the upcoming quarter.
spanish: Me gustaría compartir una actualización rápida sobre nuestros costos de adquisición de clientes para este último trimestre.

text: Let's keep the discussion focused on the project milestones and avoid discussing future product {{c1::features}}.
explanation: A 'feature' is a distinctive attribute or aspect of a software application (e.g. user authentication, dark mode).
usage:
Pattern: product features / new features
  • The developer is coding the new search feature.
  • Users requested a data export feature.
spanish: Mantengamos la discusión enfocada en los hitos del proyecto y evitemos discutir futuras características del producto.

text: Thank you all for your feedback today; we will use these insights to refine the project design before our next {{c1::sync}}.
explanation: A 'sync' (synchronization) is business jargon for a regular meeting scheduled to align team members on project updates.
usage:
Pattern: weekly sync / schedule a sync
  • Let's schedule a quick sync call tomorrow morning.
  • The weekly sync is scheduled for Tuesdays at 10:00 AM.
spanish: Gracias a todos por sus comentarios de hoy; utilizaremos estos conocimientos para refinar el diseño del proyecto antes de nuestra próxima reunión de sincronización.

text: Let's not {{c1::get bogged down in}} the implementation details during this high-level sync.
explanation: get bogged down in means to become so stuck or overwhelmed by small details that you lose sight of the main goal and stop making progress.
usage:
Pattern: get bogged down in [details/logistics/work]
  • Don't get bogged down in minor edits; focus on completing the draft.
  • Our discussion got bogged down in budget arguments.
spanish: No nos empantanemos en los detalles de implementación durante esta sincronización de alto nivel.

text: Welcome everyone; let's start by reviewing the agenda for today's call and identifying our {{c1::key objectives}}.
explanation: 'Key objectives' are the primary, most important goals or outcomes that a meeting or project is designed to achieve.
usage:
Pattern: key objectives / main goals
  • The key objectives of this phase are database cleanup and indexing.
  • What are the key objectives for today's meeting?
spanish: Bienvenidos todos; comencemos revisando la agenda para la llamada de hoy e identificando nuestros objetivos clave.

text: I'll be facilitating the discussion today, and I want to make sure everyone has an opportunity to {{c1::voice their opinion}}.
explanation: To 'voice your opinion' means to express your thoughts, beliefs, or views on a subject openly and publicly.
usage:
Pattern: voice opinion / express views
  • Employees are encouraged to voice their opinions in team surveys.
  • He was not afraid to voice his opinion during the board meeting.
spanish: Estaré moderando la discusión hoy, y quiero asegurarme de que todos tengan la oportunidad de expresar su opinión.

text: Let's keep an eye on the time: we need to move on to the next topic on the agenda in {{c1::five minutes}}.
explanation: To 'keep an eye on the time' means to monitor the clock continuously to ensure the meeting doesn't run late.
usage:
Pattern: keep an eye on the time / track time
  • We must keep an eye on the time to avoid missing our flight.
  • I am keeping an eye on the time so we finish before noon.
spanish: Estemos atentos al tiempo: necesitamos pasar al siguiente tema de la agenda en cinco minutos.

text: Could you please keep your updates concise so we have enough time to discuss the new {{c1::marketing campaign}}?
explanation: A 'marketing campaign' is an organized, strategic course of action to promote a product, service, or brand across media channels.
usage:
Pattern: marketing campaign / advertising campaign
  • We are launching a new marketing campaign next week.
  • The marketing campaign resulted in a 30% increase in traffic.
spanish: ¿Podría por favor mantener sus actualizaciones breves para que tengamos suficiente tiempo para discutir la nueva campaña de marketing?

text: Let's summarize the main points we've agreed on before we adjourn the meeting for {{c1::today}}.
explanation: To 'adjourn the meeting' is formal language meaning to temporarily end or suspend a meeting or assembly.
usage:
Pattern: adjourn the meeting / end the session
  • The committee decided to adjourn the meeting until next Tuesday.
  • The chairman adjourned the meeting at 4:00 PM.
spanish: Resumamos los puntos principales en los que hemos estado de acuerdo antes de levantar la sesión por hoy.

text: If there are no objections, let's approve the previous meeting minutes and move to the {{c1::first item}}.
explanation: To 'approve the minutes' is the formal process in business meetings of verifying that the written record of the previous meeting is accurate.
usage:
Pattern: approve the minutes / review minutes
  • The board voted to approve the minutes of the January meeting.
  • First, we must approve the minutes before discussing new business.
spanish: Si no hay objeciones, aprobemos la minuta de la reunión anterior y pasemos al primer punto.

text: I'd like to invite our guest speaker to share their insights on industry trends and customer {{c1::behavior}}.
explanation: To 'share insights' means to explain deep understandings, professional knowledge, or analytical conclusions about a topic to others.
usage:
Pattern: share insights / offer recommendations
  • The analyst shared valuable insights on market growth.
  • Thank you for sharing your insights with us today.
spanish: Me gustaría invitar a nuestro orador invitado a compartir sus conocimientos/perspectivas sobre las tendencias de la industria y el comportamiento del cliente.

text: Let's stay focused on the topic at hand; we can discuss other issues during our next scheduled {{c1::sync call}}.
explanation: The 'topic at hand' is the specific subject, problem, or agenda item currently being discussed or addressed.
usage:
Pattern: topic at hand / current subject
  • Please limit your questions to the topic at hand.
  • The manager guided the team back to the topic at hand.
spanish: Mantengámonos enfocados en el tema en cuestión; podemos discutir otros asuntos durante nuestra próxima llamada de sincronización programada.

text: Thank you for your active participation today; I will send out a follow-up email with the next steps {{c1::shortly}}.
explanation: 'Active participation' refers to actively engaging, speaking, and contributing value during a group meeting or workshop.
usage:
Pattern: active participation / engage in discussion
  • The success of the brainstorming session depended on everyone's active participation.
  • Thank you all for your active participation today.
spanish: Gracias por su participación activa hoy; enviaré un correo electrónico de seguimiento con los siguientes pasos en breve.

text: Let's start the meeting on time; we have a lot of topics to cover on our agenda {{c1::today}}.
explanation: 'Today' refers to the present day, commonly used to set context at the start of a business call.
usage:
Pattern: on our agenda today / schedule for today
  • What is the main topic on our agenda today?
  • We completed all tasks scheduled for today.
spanish: Comencemos la reunión a tiempo; tenemos muchos temas que cubrir en nuestra agenda hoy.

text: I'll be leading the meeting today; please let me know if anyone has any updates to add to the {{c1::agenda}}.
explanation: An 'agenda' is a list of items or topics to be discussed or resolved during a formal meeting.
usage:
Pattern: meeting agenda / items on the agenda
  • Please review the agenda before the call starts.
  • The manager added two new points to the meeting agenda.
spanish: Estaré guiando la reunión hoy; por favor, avísenme si alguien tiene alguna actualización que agregar a la agenda.

text: Let's keep our updates brief so we have enough time to discuss the database migration {{c1::plan}}.
explanation: A 'plan' is a detailed proposal for doing or achieving something, here referencing the technical migration plan.
usage:
Pattern: migration plan / project plan
  • The architect drafted a detailed migration plan.
  • We must follow the rollout plan to avoid service disruption.
spanish: Mantengamos nuestras actualizaciones breves para que tengamos suficiente tiempo para discutir el plan de migración de la base de datos.

text: Could you please summarize your findings in two sentences to help us stay on {{c1::schedule}}?
explanation: To 'stay on schedule' is the business collocation meaning to complete tasks or run meetings within the planned timetable.
usage:
Pattern: stay on schedule / keep on schedule
  • We adjusted the timeline to ensure we stay on schedule.
  • The project manager is responsible for keeping the team on schedule.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor resumir tus hallazgos en dos oraciones para ayudarnos a mantenernos según lo programado?

text: Let's review the action items we agreed on before we close the meeting for {{c1::this afternoon}}?
explanation: 'This afternoon' refers to the latter part of the present day, commonly set as a deadline or closure time for calls.
usage:
Pattern: this afternoon / late afternoon
  • The migration is scheduled for this afternoon.
  • I will send the report this afternoon.
spanish: ¿Revisamos las tareas pendientes en las que estuvimos de acuerdo antes de cerrar la reunión de esta tarde?

text: If there are no objections, let's approve the previous meeting minutes and move to our first {{c1::topic}}.
explanation: A 'topic' is a subject or theme of discussion or study in a meeting.
usage:
Pattern: first topic / main topic / subject of discussion
  • The first topic on our agenda is database latency.
  • Let's move to the next topic.
spanish: Si no hay objeciones, aprobemos la minuta de la reunión anterior y pasemos a nuestro primer tema.

text: I'd like to invite our product manager to present the new features scheduled for the next product {{c1::release}}.
explanation: A 'release' (or deployment) is the distribution of a new version of software to production.
usage:
Pattern: product release / next release
  • We are coding the features for the next release.
  • The code is scheduled for the upcoming release.
spanish: Me gustaría invitar a nuestro gerente de producto a presentar las nuevas características programadas para el próximo lanzamiento del producto.

text: Let's stay focused on the project milestones; we can discuss other issues in our weekly {{c1::check-in call}}.
explanation: A 'check-in call' (or status sync) is a regular, brief meeting to monitor progress and coordinate tasks.
usage:
Pattern: weekly check-in call / daily check-in
  • The manager schedules a check-in call every Monday.
  • Let's use the check-in call to coordinate our updates.
spanish: Mantengámonos enfocados en los hitos del proyecto; podemos discutir otros asuntos en nuestra llamada de control semanal.

text: Thank you all for joining; I will send out a follow-up email with the meeting notes and action items {{c1::shortly}}.
explanation: 'Shortly' is an adverb meaning in a short time from now; soon, indicating rapid follow-up action.
usage:
Pattern: shortly after / shortly before / arrive shortly
  • The service was restored shortly after the reboot.
  • The manager will join the call shortly.
spanish: Gracias a todos por unirse; enviaré un correo electrónico de seguimiento con las notas de la reunión y las tareas pendientes en breve.

text: If anyone has any remaining questions, please post them on our Slack channel to avoid running {{c1::over time}}.
explanation: To run 'over time' (or exceed schedule) is the phrasal verb meaning to take longer than the allocated meeting duration.
usage:
Pattern: running over time / exceed time limits
  • We must end the call now to avoid running over time.
  • The workshop ran over time by thirty minutes.
spanish: Si alguien tiene alguna pregunta restante, por favor publíquela en nuestro canal de Slack para evitar pasarnos de tiempo.

text: When a customer is upset about an outage, you can say: "I completely {{c1::understand how frustrating}} it must be to {{c2::have your workflow disrupted}}."
explanation: This phrase validates the customer's feelings and shows you understand the impact of the issue on their business, which immediately lowers tension.
usage:
Pattern: I understand how frustrating it must be to [action/situation]
  • I understand how frustrating it must be to lose access during a launch.
  • I understand how frustrating it must be to wait for this update.
spanish: Cuando un cliente está molesto por una interrupción, puedes decir: "Entiendo completamente lo frustrante que debe ser que se interrumpa tu flujo de trabajo".

text: To reassure a panicked client, use ownership language: "I'm going to {{c1::take ownership of this issue}} and ensure we {{c2::get this sorted out for you}} as quickly as possible."
explanation: Take ownership means accepting personal responsibility for finding a solution. Get sorted out is a common idiom meaning to resolve or organize a problem.
usage:
Pattern: take ownership of [issue] / get [something] sorted out
  • Our team will take ownership of the database migration.
  • Don't worry, we'll get this sorted out by the end of the day.
spanish: Para tranquilizar a un cliente en pánico, utiliza un lenguaje de propiedad: "Voy a hacerme cargo de este problema y me aseguraré de resolverlo lo antes posible".

scenario: Support English: Framing Bad News Positive 💡
text: Instead of saying "We can't do that," frame it constructively: "While that feature is {{c1::not currently supported}}, what we can do is {{c2::set up a workaround}} using our API."
explanation: Not currently supported is a professional way to say 'impossible' or 'no'. **Set up a workaround** means finding an alternative method to achieve the same result.
usage:
Pattern: While [request] is not currently supported, what we can do is [alternative]
  • While custom domains are not currently supported, what we can do is redirect your URL.
  • Let's set up a workaround until the patch is released.
spanish: En lugar de decir "No podemos hacer eso", plantéalo de manera constructiva: "Aunque esa función no es compatible actualmente, lo que podemos hacer es configurar una solución alternativa utilizando nuestra API".

text: To transition from an apology to appreciation, say: "Thank you for {{c1::bringing this to our attention}}, and I appreciate your {{c2::patience while we investigate}}."
explanation: Bring to attention is the standard way to acknowledge a bug report or complaint. Appreciating patience is much more positive and empowering than repeatedly saying 'sorry'.
usage:
Pattern: Thank you for bringing [issue] to our attention / appreciate your patience while we [action]
  • Thank you for bringing this rendering bug to our attention.
  • We appreciate your patience while we deploy the fix.
spanish: Para pasar de una disculpa al agradecimiento, di: "Gracias por llamar nuestra atención sobre esto, y agradezco su paciencia mientras investigamos".

text: I appreciate your patience while we work to resolve this issue; I understand how important this is to your {{c1::business operations}}.
explanation: 'Business operations' refers to the core activities, systems, and processes that a company executes daily to deliver value and generate revenue.
usage:
Pattern: business operations / day-to-day operations
  • A major database crash can disrupt critical business operations.
  • We must resume business operations as quickly as possible.
spanish: Agradezco su paciencia mientras trabajamos para resolver este problema; entiendo lo importante que es esto para sus operaciones comerciales.

text: I apologize for the inconvenience this has caused you, and I am committed to making this right as {{c1::quickly as possible}}.
explanation: 'As quickly as possible' (or ASAP) is a common phrase indicating that a task should be executed with the maximum speed and urgency.
usage:
Pattern: as quickly as possible / with high urgency
  • Please restore the website connection as quickly as possible.
  • We will process your refund as quickly as possible.
spanish: Le pido disculpas por el inconveniente que esto le ha causado, y me comprometo a corregir esto tan rápido como sea posible.

text: I hear your concerns, and I will escalate this ticket to our senior engineering team for immediate {{c1::attention}}.
explanation: 'Immediate attention' refers to high-priority focus and action required immediately to resolve a critical issue or complaint.
usage:
Pattern: requires immediate attention / top priority
  • The security breach requires immediate attention from the director.
  • We will give this matter our immediate attention.
spanish: Escucho sus preocupaciones, y escalaré este ticket a nuestro equipo de ingeniería sénior para su atención inmediata.

text: Please rest assured that we are taking this matter very seriously and will keep you updated every step of the {{c1::way}}.
explanation: To keep someone updated 'every step of the way' means to provide regular, detailed updates at each milestone or phase of a process.
usage:
Pattern: every step of the way / continuous updates
  • Our support team will guide you every step of the way during migration.
  • We will keep you informed every step of the way.
spanish: Tenga la seguridad de que nos estamos tomando este asunto muy en serio y le mantendremos informado en cada paso del camino.

text: I know this is frustrating, but I will stay on the line with you until we verify that your connection is {{c1::fully restored}}.
explanation: To be 'fully restored' means that a system or network connection has returned to 100% capacity, stability, and functionality after an outage.
usage:
Pattern: fully restored / connection restored
  • The database backup is complete, and services are fully restored.
  • We will not close the ticket until service is fully restored.
spanish: Sé que esto es frustrante, pero permaneceré en la línea con usted hasta que verifiquemos que su conexión está totalmente restaurada.

text: Thank you for bringing this to our attention; your feedback helps us improve our service for {{c1::all our customers}}.
explanation: To 'bring [something] to someone's attention' means to notify them or make them aware of a problem, mistake, or issue.
usage:
Pattern: bring to attention / point out a bug
  • Thank you for bringing this calculation error to our attention.
  • He brought the database memory leak to my attention.
spanish: Gracias por llamar nuestra atención sobre esto; sus comentarios nos ayudan a mejorar nuestro servicio para todos nuestros clientes.

text: I understand how critical this issue is to your business operations, and I will do my best to {{c1::resolve it quickly}}.
explanation: To 'resolve it quickly' means solving a technical bug or customer ticket with high speed and priority.
usage:
Pattern: resolve it quickly / fast resolution
  • The support team is working hard to resolve the database crash quickly.
  • We must address the server issue and resolve it quickly.
spanish: Entiendo lo crítico que es este problema para sus operaciones comerciales, y haré todo lo posible para resolverlo rápidamente.

text: I apologize for the frustration this outage has caused; we are working around the clock to restore the {{c1::service}}.
explanation: To 'restore service' means bringing a software system, network, or server database back online after a crash.
usage:
Pattern: restore service / restore database / back online
  • The hosting company worked for hours to restore service.
  • We restored the network service after replacing the switch.
spanish: Pido disculpas por la frustración que esta interrupción ha causado; estamos trabajando las 24 horas del día para restablecer el servicio.

text: I hear your concerns about data security, and I will escalate this ticket to our security team for immediate {{c1::review}}.
explanation: A 'review' is a formal assessment of something with the intention of instituting changes if necessary (e.g. security log analysis).
usage:
Pattern: under review / security review / code review
  • The security incident is currently under review.
  • We scheduled a review of our server access policies.
spanish: Escucho sus preocupaciones sobre la seguridad de los datos, y escalaré este ticket a nuestro equipo de seguridad para una revisión inmediata.

text: Please rest assured that we are investigating this matter thoroughly and will keep you informed every step of the {{c1::way}}.
explanation: To 'keep someone informed' means providing regular updates to a client or stakeholder during an incident investigation.
usage:
Pattern: keep you informed / regular status updates
  • I will keep you informed of any database schema modifications.
  • We kept the client informed during the server migration.
spanish: Tenga la seguridad de que estamos investigando este asunto a fondo y le mantendremos informado en cada paso del camino.

text: I know this is inconvenient, but I will stay on the call with you until we verify that your system is {{c1::fully operational}}.
explanation: To be 'fully operational' means that a software platform or server is running at 100% capacity, without any bugs or latency.
usage:
Pattern: fully operational / fully functional / active
  • The billing system is now fully operational.
  • We tested the database and verified it is fully operational.
spanish: Sé que esto es inconveniente, pero permaneceré en la llamada con usted hasta que verifiquemos que su sistema está totalmente operativo.

text: Thank you for bringing this calculation error to our attention; we will fix it in our next update {{c1::patch}}.
explanation: A 'patch' is a quick update or set of code changes designed to fix a bug or patch a security vulnerability in a software release.
usage:
Pattern: deploy a patch / security patch
  • We are deploying a security patch to fix the API leak.
  • The patch resolved several display glitches.
spanish: Gracias por llamar nuestra atención sobre este error de cálculo; lo corregiremos en nuestro próximo parche de actualización.

text: I want to make sure we resolve this issue to your satisfaction; please let me know if there is anything else I can {{c1::do for you}}.
explanation: To 'do for you' is the natural customer service phrase asking if there are any other problems or tickets that need attention.
usage:
Pattern: do for you / help you with
  • Is there anything else I can do for you today, sir?
  • Please let me know what I can do for you to resolve this.
spanish: Quiero asegurarme de que resolvamos este problema a su satisfacción; por favor, avíseme si hay algo más que pueda hacer por usted.

text: I appreciate your patience while we test the connection; it should be ready in less than {{c1::five minutes}}.
explanation: 'Five minutes' is a short time reference used to reassure customers that a technical operation is almost complete.
usage:
Pattern: in five minutes / five minutes remaining
  • The server reboot will complete in five minutes.
  • We have five minutes remaining on this call.
spanish: Agradezco su paciencia mientras probamos la conexión; debería estar lista en menos de cinco minutos.

text: I apologize for the delay in our response; our support team has been experiencing a high volume of {{c1::tickets today}}.
explanation: A 'high volume of tickets' (or high call volume) means that the customer support queue is receiving more requests than usual, causing delays.
usage:
Pattern: high volume of tickets / call volume spike
  • Due to the server outage, we are experiencing a high volume of tickets today.
  • Our response times are longer due to a high volume of tickets.
spanish: Pido disculpas por la demora en nuestra respuesta; nuestro equipo de soporte ha estado experimentando un alto volumen de tickets hoy.

text: Your satisfaction is our top priority; please let us know if the temporary workaround resolves your problem {{c1::for now}}.
explanation: 'For now' is a common temporal expression meaning temporarily, at the moment, or until a permanent fix is ready.
usage:
Pattern: for now / temporarily / until then
  • Use this temporary password for now.
  • The server is stable for now, but we need to monitor it.
spanish: Su satisfacción es nuestra máxima prioridad; por favor, háganos saber si la solución temporal resuelve su problema por ahora.

text: When a customer's architectural design is flawed, you can push back gracefully by saying: "While your setup is functional, it {{c1::introduces a single point of failure}} that could {{c2::compromise high availability}} during peak traffic."
explanation: Pointing out design risks (like single points of failure) using objective technical concepts rather than telling the client they are 'wrong' avoids defensiveness and builds advisory trust.
usage:
Pattern: [Setup] introduces [risk] that could compromise [performance metric]
  • This setup introduces a single point of failure that could compromise high availability.
  • Using a single DB instance introduces a bottleneck that could compromise throughput.
spanish: Cuando el diseño arquitectónico de un cliente es defectuoso, puedes retroceder con gracia diciendo: "Aunque su configuración es funcional, introduce un punto único de falla que podría comprometer la alta disponibilidad durante los picos de tráfico".

text: To handle a client demanding a new capability as a bug fix, say: "This behavior {{c1::aligns with current system design}}; however, I will {{c2::advocate for your use case}} directly with our product team."
explanation: "Aligns with current system design" is a professional way to state that the system is not broken, while "advocate for your use case" shows you are still partner-focused.
usage:
Pattern: [Behavior] aligns with current system design; however, I will advocate for [use case]
  • The login timeout aligns with current system design; however, I will advocate for your use case of extending it.
  • Not allowing bulk deletions aligns with current system design.
spanish: Para manejar a un cliente que exige una nueva capacidad como si fuera una corrección de errores, di: "Este comportamiento se alinea con el diseño actual del sistema; sin embargo, defenderé su caso de uso directamente con nuestro equipo de producto".

text: In a Post-Mortem or Root Cause Analysis (RCA), always close by saying: "To prevent recurrence, we are {{c1::implementing automated safeguards}} and {{c2::refining our circuit breaker thresholds}}."
explanation: An RCA must culminate in concrete, systemic preventive actions. Explaining the safeguards demonstrates that the organization has learned and adapted to protect the client's operations.
usage:
Pattern: To prevent recurrence, we are implementing [preventative action]
  • To prevent recurrence, we are implementing automated safeguards and database limits.
  • We are refining our circuit breaker thresholds to prevent cascade failures.
spanish: En un análisis de causa raíz (RCA), siempre cierra diciendo: "Para evitar que vuelva a ocurrir, estamos implementando salvaguardas automatizadas y refinando los límites de nuestros interruptores automáticos".

text: To politely refuse debugging custom scripts, say: "While I can confirm our API is responding normally, {{c1::custom scripts are outside our support scope}}; however, you can reference this {{c2::sample payload}}."
explanation: Clearly states the platform's boundaries (outside our support scope) but remains helpful by providing a reference example to guide the customer.
usage:
Pattern: [Topic] is outside our support scope; however, you can reference [alternative resource]
  • Debugging custom scripts is outside our support scope; however, you can reference our sample repository.
  • Third-party libraries are outside our support scope.
spanish: Para rechazar cortésmente la depuración de scripts personalizados, di: "Aunque puedo confirmar que nuestra API está respondiendo normalmente, los scripts personalizados están fuera de nuestro alcance de soporte; sin embargo, puede consultar este ejemplo de carga útil".

text: Instead of apologizing for a delay, use appreciation to stay assertive: "Thank you for your patience while we {{c1::conducted a deep-dive review}} into your {{c2::infrastructure configuration}}."
explanation: Saying 'thank you for your patience' validates the wait while asserting that the time was spent on a high-value action (deep-dive review), preserving authority.
usage:
Pattern: Thank you for your patience while we conducted a deep-dive review into [infrastructure/database/setup]
  • Thank you for your patience while we conducted a deep-dive review into your replication logs.
  • Our team conducted a deep-dive review and resolved the memory leak.
spanish: En lugar de disculparse por un retraso, use el agradecimiento para mantener la asertividad: "Gracias por su paciencia mientras realizábamos una revisión profunda de la configuración de su infraestructura".

text: To deflect a premature escalation to management, suggest: "I want to ensure we {{c1::exhaust all immediate diagnostic paths}} before we {{c2::engage our engineering lead}}, so we can present them with complete data."
explanation: This phrase positions the delay in escalation as a benefit to the customer (ensuring they have complete data first), maintaining control of the troubleshooting flow.
usage:
Pattern: exhaust all immediate diagnostic paths before we engage [lead/expert]
  • I want to ensure we exhaust all immediate diagnostic paths before we engage our engineering lead.
  • Let's exhaust all immediate diagnostic paths before escalation.
spanish: Para desviar una escalación prematura a la gerencia, sugiera: "Quiero asegurarme de que agotemos todas las vías de diagnóstico inmediato antes de involucrar a nuestro líder de ingeniería, para que podamos presentarles datos completos".

text: To admit an error without losing credibility, state: "Our initial assessment {{c1::missed X variable}}; we have {{c2::adjusted our troubleshooting path}} to focus on Y to get you back online safely."
explanation: Directly acknowledges the new information (missed variable) and immediately pivots to the updated action plan, projecting competence and responsiveness rather than defensiveness.
usage:
Pattern: Our initial assessment missed [variable]; we have adjusted our troubleshooting path to focus on [focus]
  • Our initial assessment missed the firewall block; we have adjusted our troubleshooting path to focus on security rules.
  • We adjusted our troubleshooting path to focus on replication delays.
spanish: Para admitir un error sin perder credibilidad, declare: "Nuestra evaluación inicial pasó por alto la variable X; hemos ajustado nuestro camino de resolución de problemas para enfocarnos en Y para que vuelva a estar en línea de manera segura".

text: To pivot a call when time is running out, say: "We have 15 minutes left on our scheduled block. To make sure we {{c1::lock down next steps}} for your production deployment, let's {{c2::pivot to the action items}}."
explanation: Using "lock down next steps" and "pivot to action items" focuses the remaining time on outcomes, preventing the meeting from ending without clear deliverables.
usage:
Pattern: We have [time] left on our scheduled block. To make sure we lock down next steps for [goal], let's pivot to [action items]
  • We have 15 minutes left on our scheduled block. To make sure we lock down next steps for your production migration, let's pivot to the action items.
  • Let's make sure we lock down next steps before the meeting ends.
spanish: Para reorientar una llamada cuando se acaba el tiempo, di: "Nos quedan 15 minutos en nuestro bloque programado. Para asegurarnos de acordar los próximos pasos para su implementación en producción, pasemos a los elementos de acción".

text: To politely interrupt a client repeating the same complaint: "I hear you, and to ensure we don't {{c1::lose ground on the diagnostic work}}, let's focus on collecting the server logs."
explanation: "Lose ground on" means to lose progress already made. This redirect politely signals that repeating the complaint stops active troubleshooting.
usage:
Pattern: I hear you, and to ensure we don't lose ground on [work/diagnostics], let's focus on [next step]
  • I hear you, and to ensure we don't lose ground on the diagnostic work, let's focus on collecting the server logs.
  • We don't want to lose ground on the replication analysis.
spanish: Para interrumpir cortésmente a un cliente que repite la misma queja: "Lo escucho, y para asegurarnos de no perder terreno en el trabajo de diagnóstico, concentrémonos en recopilar los registros del servidor".

text: We need to conduct a Root Cause Analysis (RCA) to determine why the database crashed during the {{c1::peak traffic hour}}.
explanation: 'Peak traffic hour' refers to the specific time of day when a web service or network receives the highest volume of users or transactions.
usage:
Pattern: peak traffic / high load times
  • Our infrastructure must scale up automatically during peak traffic.
  • Latency spikes occur mostly during peak traffic hours.
spanish: Necesitamos realizar un Análisis de Causa Raíz (RCA) para determinar por qué la base de datos colapsó durante la hora de mayor tráfico.

text: We need to conduct a post-mortem analysis to determine the root cause of the memory leak in our production {{c1::servers}}.
explanation: A 'production server' (or prod server) is the live server hosting the application active for real users, distinct from staging or test servers.
usage:
Pattern: production servers / deploy to production
  • Do not run untested scripts directly on production servers.
  • The production servers crashed due to high CPU load.
spanish: Necesitamos realizar un análisis posterior al incidente (post-mortem) para determinar la causa raíz de la fuga de memoria en nuestros servidores de producción.

text: Our immediate focus is to apply a hotfix to restore service, then work on a long-term {{c1::architectural fix}}.
explanation: An 'architectural fix' is a fundamental redesign of database schemas, code modules, or network topologies to resolve a recurring issue permanently.
usage:
Pattern: architectural fix / permanent redesign
  • Applying a temporary workaround is not an alternative to an architectural fix.
  • The architect proposed a database partitioning model as the architectural fix.
spanish: Nuestro enfoque inmediato es aplicar un parche para restablecer el servicio y luego trabajar en una corrección arquitectónica a largo plazo.

text: The crash was caused by a database deadlock; we need to optimize our queries and transaction isolation {{c1::levels}}.
explanation: 'Transaction isolation levels' in SQL databases control the visibility of data to other concurrent transactions, preventing data corruption.
usage:
Pattern: isolation levels / read committed / serializable
  • Setting high transaction isolation levels can increase locks and cause deadlocks.
  • We set the database isolation level to Read Committed.
spanish: El colapso fue causado por un bloqueo mutuo (deadlock) de la base de datos; necesitamos optimizar nuestras consultas y niveles de aislamiento de transacciones.

text: Let's review the API response times to identify when the performance degradation began to {{c1::affect users}}.
explanation: To 'affect users' means that software bugs, latency, or outages begin to disrupt the client experience or cause errors on user devices.
usage:
Pattern: affect users / impact clients
  • The database outage did not affect users because of the fallback cache.
  • How many clients did the latency spike affect?
spanish: Revisemos los tiempos de respuesta de la API para identificar cuándo la degradación del rendimiento comenzó a afectar a los usuarios.

text: We need to negotiate the SLA details: what is the penalty if the uptime drops below the guaranteed {{c1::99.9%}}?
explanation: '99.9%' (three nines) is a standard service level agreement uptime metric, permitting no more than 8.76 hours of downtime per year.
usage:
Pattern: 99.9% uptime / 99.99% availability
  • The hosting provider committed to a 99.9% uptime guarantee.
  • Our service fell below the 99.9% threshold this month.
spanish: Necesitamos negociar los detalles del SLA: ¿cuál es la penalización si el tiempo de actividad cae por debajo del 99.9% garantizado?

text: The post-mortem report indicates that the outage could have been avoided if we had set up server resource monitoring {{c1::alerts}}.
explanation: A 'monitoring alert' is an automated notification (sent via Slack, PagerDuty, or email) triggered when system metrics (like CPU or memory) cross a threshold.
usage:
Pattern: monitoring alerts / trigger an alert
  • Configure monitoring alerts to notify the team when disk space is low.
  • The database memory leak triggered several monitoring alerts.
spanish: El informe posterior al incidente indica que la interrupción podría haberse evitado si hubiéramos configurado alertas de monitoreo de recursos del servidor.

text: Let's establish a roll-back plan before we deploy this update to production to minimize potential service {{c1::disruptions}}.
explanation: 'Service disruptions' are events where software systems or cloud networks become unavailable, causing operations to halt.
usage:
Pattern: service disruptions / business disruption
  • The network migration caused minimal service disruptions.
  • Avoid service disruptions by testing all updates in staging first.
spanish: Establezcamos un plan de reversión antes de implementar esta actualización en producción para minimizar posibles interrupciones del servicio.

text: We need to run load tests to verify that the API gateway can handle up to double our normal request {{c1::volume}}.
explanation: 'Request volume' refers to the total number of HTTP requests processed by a web server or gateway within a specific timeframe.
usage:
Pattern: request volume / traffic volume / concurrent requests
  • Our API gateway handles a high request volume during marketing events.
  • The server crashed because the request volume exceeded its limits.
spanish: Necesitamos realizar pruebas de carga para verificar que la pasarela API pueda manejar hasta el doble de nuestro volumen de solicitudes normal.

text: The root cause was a configuration mismatch between the staging and production environment {{c1::variables}}.
explanation: 'Environment variables' are dynamic-valued objects stored in the operating system that define configuration values (like DB paths, keys) for running processes.
usage:
Pattern: environment variables / config variables
  • Make sure you do not commit environment variables containing secrets.
  • The app crashed because the database URL environment variable was missing.
spanish: La causa raíz fue una discrepancia de configuración entre las variables de entorno de los entornos de prueba (staging) y producción.

text: We will document our findings and remediation steps in a formal RCA report and share it with the client {{c1::tomorrow}}.
explanation: 'Tomorrow' is the day after today, set here as the target deadline to share the Root Cause Analysis report with stakeholders.
usage:
Pattern: share it tomorrow / report tomorrow
  • The slides are ready; we will share the report tomorrow.
  • I will email you the updates tomorrow.
spanish: Documentaremos nuestros hallazgos y pasos de remediación en un informe formal de RCA y lo compartiremos con el cliente mañana.

text: Would you like to {{c1::grab a drink}} sometime?
explanation: grab a drink is a casual, friendly way to invite someone to go out for a beverage (coffee, beer, or cocktails).
usage:
Pattern: grab a [item] (e.g. grab a coffee, grab a bite to eat, grab lunch)
  • Let's grab a coffee this afternoon.
  • Do you want to grab a bite to eat after work?
spanish: ¿Te gustaría ir a tomar algo alguna vez?

text: I had a great time tonight, we should {{c1::do this again}}.
explanation: do this again is a friendly, low-pressure phrase used at the end of a good social gathering or date to show interest in hanging out again.
usage:
Pattern: we should do this again [sometime/soon]
  • I really enjoyed the museum. We should do this again sometime!
  • It was great catching up. We should do this again soon.
spanish: La pasé genial esta noche, deberíamos repetir esto.

text: Would you like to go out for a cup of coffee sometime next week, or perhaps grab a {{c1::bite to eat}}?
explanation: To 'grab a bite to eat' is a highly common, informal idiom meaning to get a quick meal or snack together.
usage:
Pattern: grab a bite to eat / get lunch
  • Let's grab a bite to eat before the movie starts.
  • I only have fifteen minutes to grab a bite to eat.
spanish: ¿Te gustaría salir a tomar una taza de café en algún momento de la próxima semana, o tal vez comer algo rápido?

text: I had a wonderful time tonight; thank you for showing me your favorite spot in the {{c1::city}}.
explanation: A 'favorite spot' refers to a person's preferred place (like a cafe, park view, or hidden bar) for leisure or dining.
usage:
Pattern: favorite spot / local hangout
  • This coffee shop is my favorite spot for reading.
  • She showed me her favorite spot in the botanical gardens.
spanish: La pasé de maravilla esta noche; gracias por mostrarme tu lugar favorito en la ciudad.

text: Let me know when you are free; I'd love to take you out to that new Italian restaurant we talked {{c1::about}}.
explanation: To 'talk about' something is the phrasal verb meaning to discuss a topic or place in conversation.
usage:
Pattern: talk about [place / topic]
  • This is the book I was talking about yesterday.
  • We talked about the database design for hours.
spanish: Avísame cuando estés libre; me encantaría llevarte a ese nuevo restaurante italiano del que hablamos.

text: I really enjoy talking to you; we seem to have a lot of common interests and share a similar {{c1::sense of humor}}.
explanation: A 'sense of humor' is a person's capability to perceive, appreciate, or express what is funny or amusing.
usage:
Pattern: sense of humor / witty / dry humor
  • He has a dry sense of humor that is hard to understand at first.
  • A shared sense of humor is key to a good relationship.
spanish: Realmente disfruto hablar contigo; parece que tenemos muchos intereses en común y compartimos un sentido del humor similar.

text: Are you free this weekend, or do you already have plans with your {{c1::friends}}?
explanation: To 'have plans' means to have pre-arranged activities or commitments with friends or family.
usage:
Pattern: have plans / busy this weekend
  • I'd love to come, but I already have plans.
  • Do you have plans for the holidays?
spanish: ¿Estás libre este fin de semana, o ya tienes planes con tus amigos?

text: I'd love to see you again; let's keep in touch and schedule something for next {{c1::weekend}}.
explanation: To 'keep in touch' is an idiom meaning to maintain communication or contact with someone over time.
usage:
Pattern: keep in touch / stay in contact
  • We promised to keep in touch after graduation.
  • Let's keep in touch via email.
spanish: Me encantaría verte de nuevo; mantengámonos en contacto y programemos algo para el próximo fin de semana.

text: Thank you for the dinner; it was very kind of you to treat me, and I'd like to pay next {{c1::time}}.
explanation: To 'pay next time' is a polite dating convention indicating that the recipient of a free meal wants to reciprocate by paying for the next date.
usage:
Pattern: pay next time / my treat next time
  • You paid today, so let me pay next time.
  • She insisted on paying next time.
spanish: Gracias por la cena; fue muy amable de tu parte invitarme, y me gustaría pagar la próxima vez.

text: Let's go for a walk in the park; it's a beautiful day and a great opportunity to chat and get to {{c1::know each other better}}.
explanation: To 'get to know each other better' means to learn more about a person's life, opinions, and character through conversation and shared experiences.
usage:
Pattern: get to know each other better
  • Dating is a process of getting to know each other better.
  • I want to get to know my teammates better.
spanish: Vamos a dar un paseo por el parque; es un hermoso día y una gran oportunidad para charlar y conocernos mejor.

text: Would you like to grab a drink after work this Friday? I know a great little place near the {{c1::office}}.
explanation: The 'office' is the physical workplace where colleagues work, commonly used as a reference point for post-work socializing.
usage:
Pattern: near the office / head to the office
  • Let's meet at the pub near the office.
  • I am working from the office today.
spanish: ¿Te gustaría ir a tomar un trago después del trabajo este viernes? Conozco un gran lugarcito cerca de la oficina.

text: I had a wonderful time chatting with you tonight; thank you for the lovely conversation and the {{c1::company}}.
explanation: 'Company' in this social context refers to the presence of another person or group, here appreciated at the end of a date.
usage:
Pattern: enjoy the company / keep someone company
  • Thank you for keeping me company while I waited for the train.
  • I really enjoy your company.
spanish: Me divertí mucho charlando contigo esta noche; gracias por la agradable conversación y la compañía.

text: Let me know when you are free next week; I'd love to take you to that new art gallery that just {{c1::opened}}.
explanation: To 'open' (or be newly opened) is the verb describing a new business, exhibition, or venue welcoming the public for the first time.
usage:
Pattern: just opened / newly opened
  • A new coffee shop just opened around the corner.
  • The museum opened its doors to the public yesterday.
spanish: Avísame cuando estés libre la próxima semana; me encantaría llevarte a esa nueva galería de arte que acaba de abrir.

text: I really enjoy spending time with you; you are very easy to talk to and have a great {{c1::personality}}.
explanation: A 'personality' is the combination of characteristics, qualities, and behaviors that form an individual's distinctive character.
usage:
Pattern: great personality / outgoing personality
  • She has a very warm and outgoing personality.
  • I like him because of his great personality.
spanish: Realmente disfruto pasar tiempo contigo; es muy fácil hablar contigo y tienes una gran personalidad.

text: Are you busy this weekend, or do you have some free time to hang out and grab a {{c1::bite to eat}}?
explanation: To 'grab a bite to eat' is a common informal idiom meaning to get a quick meal together, used here to invite someone on a casual date.
usage:
Pattern: grab a bite to eat / get food
  • Let's grab a bite to eat after the movie.
  • I need to grab a quick bite to eat.
spanish: ¿Estás ocupado este fin de semana, o tienes algo de tiempo libre para pasar el rato y comer algo rápido?

text: I'd love to see you again soon; let's keep in touch and plan something for next {{c1::Saturday}}.
explanation: 'Saturday' is the first day of the weekend, the most common day for arranging social dates or events.
usage:
Pattern: next Saturday / Saturday night
  • Are you free next Saturday afternoon?
  • We went to a concert last Saturday.
spanish: Me encantaría verte de nuevo pronto; mantengámonos en contacto y planifiquemos algo para el próximo sábado.

text: Thank you for the coffee; it was very nice of you to pay, and I'll treat you next {{c1::time}}.
explanation: To 'treat' someone next 'time' means to pay for their drink, meal, or ticket on the subsequent occasion as a reciprocal gesture.
usage:
Pattern: treat you next time / my treat next time
  • Put your wallet away; it's my treat today, and you can treat me next time.
  • She treated us to dinner yesterday.
spanish: Gracias por el café; fue muy amable de tu parte pagar, y yo te invito la próxima vez.

text: Let's go for a walk by the river; it's a beautiful evening and a nice opportunity to chat and get to {{c1::know each other better}}.
explanation: To 'get to know each other better' is the standard phrase for learning more about a romantic partner's background, tastes, and values.
usage:
Pattern: get to know each other better / build a connection
  • We spent the evening talking and getting to know each other better.
  • Our goal is to get to know our clients better.
spanish: Vamos a dar un paseo junto al río; es una hermosa tarde y una buena oportunidad para charlar y conocernos mejor.

text: I'll text you when I get home to let you know I arrived safely; thank you for a wonderful {{c1::date}}.
explanation: A 'date' is a pre-arranged social or romantic meeting, especially between two people who are interested in each other.
usage:
Pattern: first date / go on a date
  • Our first date was at a small sushi restaurant.
  • He asked her out on a date.
spanish: Te enviaré un mensaje de texto cuando llegue a casa para avisarte que llegué bien; gracias por una cita maravillosa.

text: Would you like to join me for a concert next month? I have an extra ticket and would love for you to {{c1::come along}}.
explanation: To 'come along' is a phrasal verb meaning to accompany someone, go with them, or join them on a trip or event.
usage:
Pattern: come along / join in
  • We are going to the beach; do you want to come along?
  • She invited me to come along to the gallery.
spanish: ¿Te gustaría acompañarme a un concierto el próximo mes? Tengo una entrada extra y me encantaría que me acompañaras.

text: Explaining the game: "You must follow the {{c1::turn order}}, and all spells placed on the {{c2::stack resolve in reverse order}} of play."
explanation: Turn order is the sequence in which players act. **Stack** is a programming/game term where the last card played resolves first (Last-In, First-Out).
usage:
Pattern: follow the turn order / resolve on the stack
  • Please wait for your turn in the turn order.
  • His counter-spell resolves first because it's on top of the stack.
spanish: Explicando el juego: "Debes seguir el orden de los turnos, y todos los hechizos colocados en la pila se resuelven en orden inverso al de juego".

text: Analyzing the match: "Our defense kept a {{c1::clean sheet}}, but we failed to capitalize on the {{c2::counter-attack}} opportunities."
explanation: Clean sheet is a sports idiom meaning the opponent scored zero goals. **Counter-attack** refers to an immediate, fast attack launched in response to an opponent's offensive move.
usage:
Pattern: keep a clean sheet / launch a counter-attack
  • The goalkeeper made three saves to keep a clean sheet.
  • We scored the winning goal during a rapid counter-attack.
spanish: Analizando el partido: "Nuestra defensa mantuvo la portería a cero, pero no logramos capitalizar las oportunidades de contraataque".

text: Reviewing the saga: "The trilogy features incredible {{c1::world-building}}, but the main protagonist's {{c2::character arc}} felt rushed."
explanation: World-building is the process of constructing an imaginary, detailed fictional world. **Character arc** is the developmental journey of a character over the course of a story.
usage:
Pattern: immersive world-building / character arc development
  • Tolkien's novels are famous for their detailed world-building.
  • Her character arc shows a transition from villain to hero.
spanish: Revisando la saga: "La trilogía presenta una construcción de mundo increíble, pero el arco de personaje del protagonista principal se sintió apresurado".

text: Declining the invite: "I'd love to go, but I'm completely {{c1::swamped with work}} this week, so I'll have to {{c2::take a rain check}}."
explanation: Swamped with work means extremely busy. **Take a rain check** is an idiom meaning to politely decline an invitation now but suggest rescheduling for a later date.
usage:
Pattern: be swamped with [tasks] / take a rain check on [event]
  • I'm completely swamped with work before the release.
  • Can I take a rain check on lunch today? I have a client meeting.
spanish: Rechazando la invitación: "Me encantaría ir, pero estoy completamente abrumado de trabajo esta semana, así que tendré que dejarlo para otra ocasión".

text: Comforting the colleague: "I can't imagine {{c1::what you're going through}}, but please know you can always {{c2::lean on me}} for support."
explanation: What you're going through acknowledges their unique pain. **Lean on me** is a comforting idiom meaning to rely on someone for emotional support.
usage:
Pattern: cannot imagine what [someone] is going through / lean on [someone] during [crisis]
  • I am here for you, I cannot imagine what you're going through.
  • You can lean on me for help with your tasks this week.
spanish: Consolando al colega: "No puedo imaginar por lo que estás pasando, pero por favor sabe que siempre puedes apoyarte en mí para recibir ayuda".

text: It's been a while; what have you been {{c1::up to}} lately?
explanation: To be 'up to [something]' is an informal phrasal verb meaning to do or be busy with certain activities.
usage:
Pattern: what have you been up to / what are you up to
  • What are you up to this weekend?
  • I haven't seen you in months, what have you been up to?
spanish: Ha pasado un tiempo; ¿en qué has andado metido/qué has estado haciendo últimamente?

text: Would you like to join us for a drink after work, or do you need to go straight {{c1::home}}?
explanation: To 'go straight home' means to travel directly from your current location (like work) to your house without stopping anywhere else.
usage:
Pattern: go straight home / head home
  • I am tired, so I'm going straight home.
  • He went straight home after school.
spanish: ¿Te gustaría acompañarnos a tomar un trago después del trabajo, o necesitas irte directo a casa?

text: I'm planning a small get-together at my place this Friday, and I'd love for you to {{c1::come by}}.
explanation: To 'come by' is a phrasal verb meaning to pay a casual, short visit to a person's house or location.
usage:
Pattern: come by / drop by / stop by
  • You can come by my desk if you have any questions.
  • Please come by our office when you are free.
spanish: Estoy planeando una pequeña reunión en mi casa este viernes, y me encantaría que pasaras por allí.

text: Thank you so much for hosting us; you have a lovely home and are a wonderful {{c1::host}}.
explanation: A 'host' is a person who receives or entertains other people as guests in their home or at an event.
usage:
Pattern: gracious host / host a dinner
  • Our host provided us with a delicious home-cooked meal.
  • She was a wonderful host during our stay.
spanish: Muchas gracias por recibirnos; tienes una casa preciosa y eres un anfitrión maravilloso.

text: Let's keep in touch; do you use any messaging apps or are you active on {{c1::social media}}?
explanation: 'Social media' refers to interactive computer-mediated technologies (like LinkedIn, Instagram) that facilitate the creation and sharing of information.
usage:
Pattern: social media account / active on social media
  • I don't spend much time on social media.
  • Let's connect on social media.
spanish: Mantengámonos en contacto; ¿usas alguna aplicación de mensajería o estás activo en las redes sociales?

text: I haven't seen you in ages! What have you been {{c1::doing}} lately?
explanation: To 'do' (or be doing) is the standard verb for executing actions or pursuing activities, used here in a catch-up greeting.
usage:
Pattern: what have you been doing / what are you doing
  • What are you doing after work?
  • I spent the weekend doing chores.
spanish: ¡No te he visto en siglos! ¿Qué has estado haciendo últimamente?

text: Would you like to join us for dinner after work, or do you need to head straight {{c1::home}}?
explanation: 'Home' is the noun representing the physical house where a person lives, used here to check if they have post-work commitments.
usage:
Pattern: head home / go home / stay home
  • I am going to head home now; see you tomorrow.
  • We stayed home during the storm.
spanish: ¿Te gustaría acompañarnos a cenar después del trabajo, o necesitas irte directo a casa?

text: I'm hosting a small get-together at my place this Saturday; I'd love for you to {{c1::come by}}.
explanation: To 'come by' is the phrasal verb meaning to pay a casual, short visit to a person's house or office.
usage:
Pattern: come by / drop by / visit
  • You should come by our office next time you are in town.
  • She came by my desk to ask a question.
spanish: Estoy organizando una pequeña reunión en mi casa este sábado; me encantaría que pasaras por allí.

text: Thank you so much for the dinner; you have a lovely home and are a wonderful {{c1::host}}.
explanation: A 'host' is a person who receives or entertains guests in their home or at an event, providing hospitality.
usage:
Pattern: gracious host / host a party
  • The host welcomed us at the door.
  • She is an excellent host who always prepares amazing food.
spanish: Muchas gracias por la cena; tienes una casa preciosa y eres un anfitrión maravilloso.

text: Let's keep in touch; are you active on any social media or messaging {{c1::platforms}}?
explanation: A 'platform' in a social context refers to messaging or social networking applications (like WhatsApp or LinkedIn) used to communicate.
usage:
Pattern: social media platforms / messaging platforms
  • We use multiple messaging platforms to coordinate.
  • Which social media platforms do you prefer?
spanish: Mantengámonos en contacto; ¿estás activo en alguna red social o plataforma de mensajería?

text: It was great catching up with you today; let's not wait so long to meet up next {{c1::time}}.
explanation: 'Time' (in 'next time') refers to the subsequent occurrence, meeting, or event when two people will meet again.
usage:
Pattern: next time / this time / last time
  • I will treated you next time.
  • Last time we met was in Paris.
spanish: Fue genial ponerse al día contigo hoy; no esperemos tanto para reunirnos la próxima vez.

text: Could you please pass my regards to your family when you see them {{c1::this weekend}}?
explanation: 'This weekend' refers to the upcoming Saturday and Sunday, commonly mentioned when parting from someone before the workweek ends.
usage:
Pattern: this weekend / plans for the weekend
  • What are you doing this weekend?
  • I am visiting my parents this weekend.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor enviarle mis saludos a tu familia cuando los veas este fin de semana?

text: It was a pleasure talking to you; have a safe trip home and take {{c1::care}}.
explanation: To 'take care' is a very common, warm, and friendly parting phrase meaning look after yourself.
usage:
Pattern: take care / take care of yourself
  • Goodbye, take care!
  • You should take care of your health.
spanish: Fue un placer hablar contigo; que tengas un viaje seguro a casa y cuídate.

text: Would you like to grab a coffee this afternoon? I need a short break from the {{c1::office}}.
explanation: The 'office' is the physical workplace where an employee works, used here to suggest a temporary break.
usage:
Pattern: in the office / leave the office
  • I am working in the office today.
  • Let's step out of the office for a walk.
spanish: ¿Te gustaría ir a tomar un café esta tarde? Necesito un pequeño descanso de la oficina.

text: Let's plan a weekend trip to the mountains next month to escape the busy city {{c1::life}}.
explanation: 'City life' refers to the daily lifestyle, routines, and fast pace characteristic of living in a major urban center.
usage:
Pattern: city life / urban life / country life
  • I love the cultural activities in city life.
  • City life can be very stressful and noisy.
spanish: Planifiquemos un viaje de fin de semana a las montañas el próximo mes para escapar de la ajetreada vida de la ciudad.

text: Looking back: "I {{c1::vividly remember}} visiting the Andes; it was an {{c2::eye-opening experience}} that changed how I saw nature."
explanation: Vividly remember means to recall with clear, bright, and detailed memory. **Eye-opening experience** is an event that teaches you something surprising or changes your perspective.
usage:
Pattern: vividly remember [action/event] / eye-opening experience
  • I vividly remember my first coding project.
  • Working in tech support was an eye-opening experience.
spanish: Mirando hacia atrás: "Recuerdo vívidamente haber visitado los Andes; fue una experiencia reveladora que cambió mi forma de ver la naturaleza".

text: Recalling his last job: "I was {{c1::snowed under}} with tickets, and when the server crashed, I was at my {{c2::wit's end}}."
explanation: Snowed under is an idiom meaning overwhelmed with work. **At one's wit's end** is an idiom meaning completely stressed out and not knowing what to do next.
usage:
Pattern: be snowed under with [work] / at my wit's end
  • I'm completely snowed under with homework this semester.
  • I was at my wit's end trying to solve the connection bug.
spanish: Recordando su último trabajo: "Estaba inundado de tickets, y cuando el servidor se cayó, estaba al borde de la locura (no sabía qué hacer)".

text: Telling the story: "We crossed paths {{c1::out of the blue}} in Berlin, and we {{c2::hit it off}} immediately."
explanation: Out of the blue is an idiom meaning completely unexpected. **Hit it off** is a phrasal verb meaning to quickly become friendly or connect with someone.
usage:
Pattern: cross paths out of the blue / hit it off with [someone]
  • I ran into my former colleague out of the blue.
  • We hit it off during our first meeting at the conference.
spanish: Contando la historia: "Nos cruzamos por casualidad (de la nada) en Berlín, y congeniamos de inmediato".

text: Relating the emergency: "I got a {{c1::minor gash}} on my arm, but the doctor bandaged it and issued a {{c2::medical discharge}} within an hour."
explanation: Gash is a long, deep cut or wound in the skin. **Medical discharge** is the official release of a patient from medical care.
usage:
Pattern: gash on [body part] / receive a medical discharge
  • He had a deep gash on his knee after falling.
  • The hospital prepared his medical discharge papers.
spanish: Relatando la emergencia: "Me hice un corte menor en el brazo, pero el médico lo vendó y emitió un alta médica en una hora".

text: Reflecting on the project: "In {{c1::hindsight}}, the failed launch was a blessing in disguise because our {{c2::key takeaway}} was the need for automated tests."
explanation: In hindsight means looking back at an event in the past. **Blessing in disguise** is an idiom meaning something bad that turns out to have good results. **Takeaway** is the key lesson learned.
usage:
Pattern: in hindsight, [situation] was a blessing in disguise / key takeaway from [event]
  • In hindsight, losing that client helped us focus on better partners.
  • The key takeaway from the security audit was to rotate keys immediately.
spanish: Reflexionando sobre el proyecto: "En retrospectiva, el lanzamiento fallido fue una bendición disfrazada porque nuestro principal aprendizaje fue la necesidad de pruebas automatizadas".

text: To make a long story short, we ended up missing our flight and had to spend the night at the {{c1::airport terminal}}.
explanation: To 'make a long story short' is a very common idiom used to skip minor details and summarize the final outcome of an event quickly.
usage:
Pattern: to make a long story short / long story short
  • Long story short, I got the job.
  • To make a long story short, we had to walk home in the rain.
spanish: Para no hacerles el cuento largo (resumiendo), terminamos perdiendo nuestro vuelo y tuvimos que pasar la noche en la terminal del aeropuerto.

text: Believe it or not, she actually won the lottery but decided to donate all the winnings to {{c1::charity}}.
explanation: 'Believe it or not' is a conversational expression used to introduce a surprising, remarkable, or unexpected fact that is nevertheless true.
usage:
Pattern: believe it or not / surprising fact
  • Believe it or not, I've never eaten sushi.
  • He completed the marathon in two hours, believe it or not.
spanish: Lo creas o no, ella realmente ganó la lotería pero decidió donar todas las ganancias a la caridad.

text: Out of the blue, my childhood friend called me after ten years of no contact, which was a pleasant {{c1::surprise}}.
explanation: 'Out of the blue' is an idiom meaning completely unexpectedly, suddenly, or without any warning.
usage:
Pattern: out of the blue / suddenly
  • She quit her job out of the blue.
  • He showed up at my house out of the blue.
spanish: De la nada (inesperadamente), mi amigo de la infancia me llamó después de diez años sin contacto, lo cual fue una grata sorpresa.

text: To cut to the chase, we need to approve the proposal today if we want to meet our launch {{c1::deadline}}.
explanation: To 'cut to the chase' is an idiom meaning to stop talking about minor details and address the most important part of a matter directly.
usage:
Pattern: cut to the chase / get straight to point
  • Let's cut to the chase: how much will this cost?
  • The director cut to the chase and announced the layoffs.
spanish: Para ir al grano (resumiendo), necesitamos aprobar la propuesta hoy si queremos cumplir con nuestra fecha límite de lanzamiento.

text: As luck would have it, I found my lost keys right when I was about to call a locksmith to {{c1::replace them}}.
explanation: 'As luck would have it' is an idiom meaning by chance or fortune (can be used for both good and bad luck).
usage:
Pattern: as luck would have it / fortunately / unfortunately
  • As luck would have it, our train was delayed by two hours.
  • I forgot my umbrella, but as luck would have it, it didn't rain.
spanish: Como quiso la suerte (por suerte), encontré mis llaves perdidas justo cuando estaba a punto de llamar a un cerrajero para reemplazarlas.

text: To make a long story short, we missed the train and had to wait at the station for {{c1::three hours}}.
explanation: 'Three hours' is a specific duration of time, used here to complete the storytelling outcome of missing a train.
usage:
Pattern: wait for three hours / three hours remaining
  • The database backup took three hours to complete.
  • We stood in line for three hours.
spanish: Para abreviar la historia (resumiendo), perdimos el tren y tuvimos que esperar en la estación durante tres horas.

text: Believe it or not, he actually won the competition despite having never practiced the sport {{c1::before}}.
explanation: 'Before' is an adverb meaning at a previous time; in the past, completing the contrast of winning without prior experience.
usage:
Pattern: never before / day before / before yesterday
  • I have never seen this error message before.
  • Read the instructions before starting the setup.
spanish: Lo creas o no, él realmente ganó la competencia a pesar de no haber practicado nunca ese deporte antes.

text: Out of the blue, my former manager emailed me with a job offer, which was a pleasant {{c1::surprise}}.
explanation: A 'surprise' is an unexpected or astonishing event, fact, or thing, here described as a positive outcome.
usage:
Pattern: pleasant surprise / complete surprise
  • His sudden promotion was a complete surprise.
  • The nice weather was a pleasant surprise.
spanish: De la nada (inesperadamente), mi antiguo gerente me envió un correo electrónico con una oferta de trabajo, lo cual fue una grata sorpresa.

text: To cut to the chase, we need to approve the budget today if we want to meet our launch {{c1::deadline}}.
explanation: A 'deadline' is the latest time or date by which something must be completed or submitted.
usage:
Pattern: meet the deadline / miss the deadline / strict deadline
  • We must work overtime to meet the launch deadline.
  • Missing the deadline will delay the product release.
spanish: Para ir al grano (resumiendo), necesitamos aprobar el presupuesto hoy si queremos cumplir con nuestra fecha límite de lanzamiento.

text: As luck would have it, I found my passport right when I was about to call the embassy to {{c1::replace it}}.
explanation: To 'replace' something means to provide a substitute or equivalent in place of an item that was lost, damaged, or expired.
usage:
Pattern: replace it / replace device / replacement parts
  • I lost my credit card and had to call the bank to replace it.
  • We need to replace the old database servers.
spanish: Como quiso la suerte (por suerte), encontré mi pasaporte justo cuando estaba a punto de llamar a la embajada para reemplazarlo.
